content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers.

It is guaranteed that AAA contains **at least two distinct** elements.

You are also given a non-negative integer XXX.

For each YYY (0‚Č§Y‚Č§X)(0 \leq Y \leq X)(0‚Č§Y‚Č§X), we create an array BBB of size NNN such that Bi=Ai‚ą£YB_i = A_i | YBi‚Äč=Ai‚Äč‚ą£Y, where ‚ą£|‚ą£ denotes the bitwise OR operation.

Find the **largest** integer Y(Y‚Č§X)Y(Y \leq X)Y(Y‚Č§X) such that the array BBB contains **at least two distinct** elements.

**Function Description:**You are given a function named **`maximizeXor`** that you must complete.

The function has the following parameters:


**`a`** ‚Äď a vector of `N` non-negative integers
**`x`** ‚Äď a non-negative integer representing the upper bound for `Y`
The function must **return this maximum valid value of `Y`**.

The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and XXX ‚ÄĒ the length of the array AAA and maximum value of YYY allowed.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč,  representing the array AAA.
For each test case, output on a new line, the largest YYY satisfying the condition.
